source.content:
Connor O'Brien: Need to lock the runtime 1.21 canary window for kernel auto-selection v2. 

Proposal: Tue 02/03 10:00–12:00 PT (hosted canary only), then Wed 02/04 10:00–12:00 PT (expand to 2nd region if green). 

Also: can we confirm who has rollback authority + who is on point to execute? (want zero ambiguity during the window)

Talia Benson: Works for me. That window doesn’t conflict w/ the other 1.21 items. I’ll update ENG-4827 + epic w/ times once we confirm.

Sahana Iyer: CI + artifacts will be ready by Mon EOD if we cut rc2. GPU perf jobs are green on A100/H100; L40S pending one rerun (flaky runner).

Sean Gallagher: SRE oncall coverage: I can be primary for the Tue window. Wed I’m in a customer review 10:30–11:30 PT but can still jump if paged.

Allison Grant: For canary monitoring: please make sure we’re using the new dashboard panels (the old queries had cardinality issues). Link: https://grafana.redwood/serving/runtime-1-21-kernel-selection-v2

Rollback trigger guidance (for the window): 
- p99 latency +10% sustained 10m for any top-5 model in canary pool
- error rate +0.2% absolute sustained 5m
- selection distribution shift >25% in any single (model,gpu,seq_bucket) without matching perf improvement

Yuki Sato: Tue 10–12 PT is good. I can be in channel the whole time + have the selector debug flag build handy.

If we need a “fast rollback”: control-plane gate flip back to v1 (no deploy), then we can go surgical with per-model denylist after.

Ethan Park: Gate is `kernel_selection_v2` (runtime reads from config service every ~60s). We can scope by env/region + allowlist/denylist by model.

I can be online Tue 10–11:30 PT; Benji can cover after.

Benji Okafor: Yup I can cover 11:30–12 PT Tue. For rollback, I can apply denylist + hot reload config. 

Runbook step references are here: https://confluence.redwood/eng-serving-runtime/runbooks/kernel-selection-v2-override-and-rollback-runbook

Omar El-Sayed: One request: during canary, keep telemetry sampling at 1% (not 10%). We saw selector overhead increase when the event payload got large. 

If we need deep debug, we can temporarily bump sampling for a single model + region.

Connor O'Brien: Great. Let’s align on “rollback decision” vs “rollback execution.” I want a single DRI for go/no-go.

My proposal:
- Go/no-go + rollback decision DRI: Connor (release train) in consultation w/ Sean (SRE) + Yuki (runtime)
- Rollback execution DRI: Sean (or SRE oncall) for gate flip; Benji/Ethan for config rules if gate flip doesn’t fully mitigate

Any objections?

Sean Gallagher: Works. If we hit a trigger, I’ll call it out in-thread and proceed w/ gate flip unless someone explicitly blocks in <2 mins. (we can always re-enable after triage)

Allison Grant: +1. Please also agree on the “all clear” criteria before expanding to 2nd region Wed.

Suggested: 60 mins stable after enabling, and at least 2M requests in canary pool with p95/p99 within budgets.

Talia Benson: I’ll add that to the rollout checklist doc and link it here. Also, who’s writing the incident-style updates during the canary?

Connor O'Brien: I can do updates every 15 mins in-thread. If things get spicy, we switch to #incidents.

ReleaseBot: Reminder: Runtime 1.21 canary checklist status: 14/18 items complete. Missing items: (1) rollback test in staging, (2) denylist reviewed for L40S, (3) dashboard link added to release notes.

Sahana Iyer: Rollback test in staging: I can run that Monday 1pm PT. Need Ethan/Benji to confirm the config propagation timing.

Ethan Park: Propagation is ~60s, worst case 2 mins. We can validate in staging by flipping gate and watching selector metrics drop.

Benji Okafor: Denylist for L40S: current list includes (kernel=flash_v3, driver=550.54) disable. I’ll double-check against latest known-issues doc and post the final snippet.

Yuki Sato: Re: known-issues — we also want to keep the long-context prefill rule threshold from PR-28522. That’s in rc2, right?

Sahana Iyer: Yes, rc2 includes 28522 + 28495 (driver guardrail). 

Omar El-Sayed: Telemetry sampling default is 1% in canary env? pls confirm. I don’t want anyone flipping it to 10% “for visibility” during the first 30 mins.

Connor O'Brien: Confirmed: keep at 1% for first hour. If we need more, we do targeted bump with Yuki/Omar call.

Allison Grant: Also note: there was an internal alert false positive last time due to label changes. The updated dashboard panels should prevent that, but keep an eye on the alert channel.

Connor O'Brien: Ok locking schedule:
- Tue 02/03 10:00–12:00 PT: Hosted canary (1 region) enable `kernel_selection_v2` for allowlisted models only
- Wed 02/04 10:00–12:00 PT: Expand to 2nd region IF all-clear criteria met

DRIs:
- Go/no-go + rollback decision: Connor 
- Rollback execution: Sean (gate flip), Benji/Ethan (denylist/rules)
- Runtime triage: Yuki + Omar on standby

Talia please update epic + checklist. Everyone pls keep this thread pinned until Wed.

Talia Benson: Will do. Updating ENG-4827 + epic now and adding the DRIs + criteria to the checklist doc. :thumbsup:

Sean Gallagher: I’ll pre-open the dashboards + have the runbook steps ready. Will post the exact gate key + current value at start of window.

Benji Okafor: I’ll drop the denylist snippet here once reviewed so we’re not scrambling mid-canary.

Connor O'Brien: Thanks all. See you Tue 9:55 PT here for preflight.

source.body:
Connor O'Brien: looking to lock next week’s release train (Wed). can we include the cost model changes? I’m tracking:
- pr-28451 add cost-model schema package v1
- pr-28457 cost-model service internal endpoints
- pr-28462 client library (version negotiation)
plus the console switch pr-28470 is “optional” depending on risk.

Connor O'Brien: need owners to confirm (1) rollout/flagging (2) rollback plan (3) any migration/backfill required. we had a couple last-minute schema changes in other trains, so trying to avoid surprises.

Talia Benson: +1. we should be explicit on what’s going out vs what stays dark. success criteria for train should be “no customer-visible changes, no billing diffs”.

Ethan Park: from platform side: schema package + service endpoints are safe to ship. endpoints are internal-only, behind auth. no existing callers except the staging canary jobs.
Ethan Park: console integration (pr-28470) is gated behind `console_cost_model_api_enabled` and has fallback to existing aggregation path.

Chloe Martin: for Console we can hold pr-28470 if release wants to be conservative. but we’d like schema+service to land so we can start dogfooding in staging.

Jada Williams: what’s the default behavior if the cost-model service errors? (timeouts, 5xx) is it hard-fail on the request path? we’re sensitive to latency spikes.

Ethan Park: client lib defaults:
- timeout 150ms (configurable)
- retries 1 (only on safe idempotent endpoints)
- on timeout/error, console caller should fall back (we already do in pr-28470)
Also server side has request bounds, and we’ve got caching on the “estimate” path for common shapes.

Rafael Mendes: release question from SRE: are we introducing any new mandatory dependencies for Console prod traffic this train? if pr-28470 is included, it *is* a new dependency (even with fallback) and will show up in latency graphs.

Connor O'Brien: that’s what I need to decide. schema+service alone seems fine. console adoption is the risk.

Allison Grant: from observability POV, if you do ship pr-28470, please ensure it emits:
- feature flag state
- fallback reason counts
- cost_model_api latency (p50/p95) as a separate timer
so we can quickly tell if the new path is the culprit.

Chloe Martin: yep, metrics are in the PR:
`console.cost_model_api.enabled` (gauge)
`console.cost_model_api.fallback.count` (tagged by reason)
`console.cost_model_api.request_ms`

Kaitlyn Nguyen: auth/tenancy note: cost-model service checks `tenant_id` from mTLS principal + request header match. if mismatch -> 403. no “best effort” there.
Kaitlyn Nguyen: for rollback, worst case we turn off the console flag and traffic goes back to old path. service can stay up.

Cole Summers: billing hooks not part of this train, right? (pr-28521 later). just want to avoid “numbers changed” escalations.

Connor O'Brien: correct. billing alignment is in a later train. this one is schema + internal endpoints (+ maybe console flag but default off).

Logan Wright: telemetry pipeline changes (batch efficiency metrics) aren’t in this train either. cost-model service will handle missing optional fields by returning `input_quality: degraded` and omitting the breakdown subcomponents. so safe.

Marissa Cole: product risk call: please do NOT ship console enable-by-default. we need staged rollout (internal tenants only) + compare vs invoice/legacy for at least a week.

Connor O'Brien: ok. proposal:
Train includes pr-28451, pr-28457, pr-28462.
Train does NOT include pr-28470 unless everyone signs off by EOD.
If we include pr-28470, it ships with flag default OFF in all envs.

Ethan Park: +1.

Chloe Martin: ok with excluding pr-28470 from train if needed. but if included w/ flag off, that’s also fine.

Rafael Mendes: if pr-28470 is included even with flag off, I want a clear rollback step in the release notes: disable flag + verify fallback rate returns to baseline. also ensure we can hotfix revert without schema breakage.

Jada Williams: +1. and please confirm: schema changes are additive only? console old path shouldn’t care, but I don’t want any shared types changes to break compilation.

Ethan Park: schema v1 package is additive new module; no existing types removed. version negotiation is “client sends `x-cost-model-schema: v1`”, server defaults to latest compatible. unknown fields ignored.

Allison Grant: any data migration? (db, cache keys) that would make rollback messy?

Ethan Park: no. service reads from existing model catalog + telemetry store. no persistent writes beyond ephemeral cache.

DeployBot: PR #28451 merged into main (commit 9b3a7c1)

Connor O'Brien: thanks. can we confirm pr-28457 + pr-28462 are ready to merge before code freeze tomorrow?

Ethan Park: pr-28457 is in review, last open item is tightening error codes per API guidelines (400 vs 422). should be merged today.

Kaitlyn Nguyen: pr-28462 I approved pending one change: client must include request id header for tracing. small.

Talia Benson: can we capture explicit “go/no-go” criteria for the train? suggested:
- cost-model service p95 < 120ms in staging under synthetic load
- error rate < 0.5% (excluding 4xx)
- no unexpected schema version distribution (should be mostly v1)

Rafael Mendes: and please add: if service is degraded, we do NOT page Console oncall; cost-model oncall owns. (so routes in pager config are correct)

Connor O'Brien: ok, action items:
1) Ethan merge pr-28457 today; Kaitlyn/Ethan merge pr-28462 today.
2) Chloe decide whether to include pr-28470; if included, confirm flag default OFF + metrics.
3) Rafael add a short rollback snippet to release notes.
I’ll mark train as “schema+service only” unless we get explicit signoff on console PR.

Chloe Martin: signoff from Console: we don’t need pr-28470 in this train. we’ll land it next train once service is stable and dashboards are live.

Connor O'Brien: perfect. locking train scope: pr-28451, pr-28457, pr-28462 only.

DeployBot: PR #28457 merged into main (commit 3f1d0ad)
DeployBot: PR #28462 merged into main (commit 5c2e11b)

Connor O'Brien: great. I’ll add to release notes:
- internal cost model API v1 endpoints (not enabled for product surfaces)
- schema package v1
- client library with schema version negotiation
Rollback: no action required; no callers. if any unexpected issues, disable service via env toggle and/or revert deploy; no migrations.

Rafael Mendes: please add: if we do disable service, ensure it returns fast 503 (not hanging) so callers can fail fast.

Ethan Park: ack, we have `FAIL_CLOSED=true` behavior (immediate 503) if downstream telemetry store is unhealthy. we’ll double-check before release.

Connor O'Brien: cool. closing this thread unless new blockers.

- feature flag state
- fallback reason counts
- cost_model_api latency (p50/p95) as a separate timer
so we can quickly tell if the new path is the culprit.

Chloe Martin: yep, metrics are in the PR:
`console.cost_model_api.enabled` (gauge)
`console.cost_model_api.fallback.count` (tagged by reason)
`console.cost_model_api.request_ms`

Kaitlyn Nguyen: auth/tenancy note: cost-model service checks `tenant_id` from mTLS principal + request header match. if mismatch -> 403. no “best effort” there.
Kaitlyn Nguyen: for rollback, worst case we turn off the console flag and traffic goes back to old path. service can stay up.

Cole Summers: billing hooks not part of this train, right? (pr-28521 later). just want to avoid “numbers changed” escalations.

Connor O'Brien: correct. billing alignment is in a later train. this one is schema + internal endpoints (+ maybe console flag but default off).

Logan Wright: telemetry pipeline changes (batch efficiency metrics) aren’t in this train either. cost-model service will handle missing optional fields by returning `input_quality: degraded` and omitting the breakdown subcomponents. so safe.

Marissa Cole: product risk call: please do NOT ship console enable-by-default. we need staged rollout (internal tenants only) + compare vs invoice/legacy for at least a week.

Connor O'Brien: ok. proposal:
Train includes pr-28451, pr-28457, pr-28462.
Train does NOT include pr-28470 unless everyone signs off by EOD.
If we include pr-28470, it ships with flag default OFF in all envs.

Ethan Park: +1.

Chloe Martin: ok with excluding pr-28470 from train if needed. but if included w/ flag off, that’s also fine.

Rafael Mendes: if pr-28470 is included even with flag off, I want a clear rollback step in the release notes: disable flag + verify fallback rate returns to baseline. also ensure we can hotfix revert without schema breakage.

Jada Williams: +1. and please confirm: schema changes are additive only? console old path shouldn’t care, but I don’t want any shared types changes to break compilation.

Ethan Park: schema v1 package is additive new module; no existing types removed. version negotiation is “client sends `x-cost-model-schema: v1`”, server defaults to latest compatible. unknown fields ignored.

Allison Grant: any data migration? (db, cache keys) that would make rollback messy?

Ethan Park: no. service reads from existing model catalog + telemetry store. no persistent writes beyond ephemeral cache.

DeployBot: PR #28451 merged into main (commit 9b3a7c1)

Connor O'Brien: thanks. can we confirm pr-28457 + pr-28462 are ready to merge before code freeze tomorrow?

Ethan Park: pr-28457 is in review, last open item is tightening error codes per API guidelines (400 vs 422). should be merged today.

Kaitlyn Nguyen: pr-28462 I approved pending one change: client must include request id header for tracing. small.

Talia Benson: can we capture explicit “go/no-go” criteria for the train? suggested:
- cost-model service p95 < 120ms in staging under synthetic load
- error rate < 0.5% (excluding 4xx)
- no unexpected schema version distribution (should be mostly v1)

Rafael Mendes: and please add: if service is degraded, we do NOT page Console oncall; cost-model oncall owns. (so routes in pager config are correct)

Connor O'Brien: ok, action items:
1) Ethan merge pr-28457 today; Kaitlyn/Ethan merge pr-28462 today.
2) Chloe decide whether to include pr-28470; if included, confirm flag default OFF + metrics.
3) Rafael add a short rollback snippet to release notes.
I’ll mark train as “schema+service only” unless we get explicit signoff on console PR.

Chloe Martin: signoff from Console: we don’t need pr-28470 in this train. we’ll land it next train once service is stable and dashboards are live.

Connor O'Brien: perfect. locking train scope: pr-28451, pr-28457, pr-28462 only.

DeployBot: PR #28457 merged into main (commit 3f1d0ad)
DeployBot: PR #28462 merged into main (commit 5c2e11b)

Connor O'Brien: great. I’ll add to release notes:
- internal cost model API v1 endpoints (not enabled for product surfaces)
- schema package v1
- client library with schema version negotiation
Rollback: no action required; no callers. if any unexpected issues, disable service via env toggle and/or revert deploy; no migrations.

Rafael Mendes: please add: if we do disable service, ensure it returns fast 503 (not hanging) so callers can fail fast.

Ethan Park: ack, we have `FAIL_CLOSED=true` behavior (immediate 503) if downstream telemetry store is unhealthy. we’ll double-check before release.

Connor O'Brien: cool. closing this thread unless new blockers.

source.text:
Connor O'Brien: Requesting a temporary release freeze for serving runtime rollouts until perf-canary signals stabilize + we finish threshold tuning. We had 2 warning spikes yesterday + 1 near-page this AM that look like baseline drift / ingestion lag, not real perf.

Connor O'Brien: Proposal:
- Freeze: runtime + kernel rollouts to prod (hosted + dedicated) for 48h
- Allowed: Sev1/Sev2 fixes w/ explicit signoff from SRE + runtime TL
- Goal: reduce overlapping changes while we tune min-samples/dampening + region/SKU thresholds

Connor O'Brien: Context links:
- Incident thread: https://slack.redwood.example/archives/incidents/p1769034455
- Canary baseline drift discussion: https://slack.redwood.example/archives/eng-runtime/p1768604412
- Current rollout schedule sheet: https://drive.redwood.example/eng-releases/runtime-rollouts

Noah Patel: +1 from runtime. The canary regression evaluator is too twitchy in us-east right now. We’re pushing a config change today to bump min sample size and add a 15m hold before escalating warning->page.

Allison Grant: To be clear, are we freezing only the runtime deploy train, or also telemetry/schema changes? Some of the noise is from metrics pipeline latency, so freezing runtime alone might not help.

Connor O'Brien: Good q. My intent is specifically changes that can affect latency/tps (runtime build, kernels, scheduler config, fleet placement). Telemetry/schema changes can proceed but please avoid breaking dashboards/alerts.

Sean Gallagher: SRE vote: freeze is reasonable. Oncall got a bunch of low-signal noise this week and it’s hard to tell what’s real vs baseline wobble. We should also lock the canary target model list during the freeze.

Vanessa Ortiz: Fleet side: we have a planned GPU driver patch in eu-west tonight. That could move perf a bit. Do we want to pause that too? It’s “infra” but impacts canary.

Connor O'Brien: If it moves perf and canary can’t attribute yet, we should defer unless it’s urgent. Can you check if we can slip 48h?

Vanessa Ortiz: I can slip it to Friday. Not urgent.

Hiro Tanaka: Kernel team had a minor kernel selection tweak queued (auto-select fallback ordering). Not merged yet. Happy to hold.

Sofia Ivanova: Scheduler change (batching heuristic) is in staging only; was tentatively going to prod Thu. We can pause. I’d prefer the freeze includes scheduler flags too (we’ve seen queueing-time shift look like latency regression).

Logan Wright: Re: telemetry pipeline, we’re debugging ingestion delay in us-east. Worst-case 8–12m lag during peak. That made “observed window” mismatched vs baseline. We have a fix behind a feature flag and can roll it safely (it’s just buffering/flush). Not performance-impacting for inference.

Allison Grant: +1 to allow telemetry fixes. But we should call out: any telemetry change needs to keep label cardinality stable. Last week we almost exploded series count.

Rafael Mendes: From paging hygiene side: thank you. The near-page this AM was painful—looked like p95 +18% on a top model, but trace links were empty b/c metrics were late. Freeze helps us not chase ghosts.

Marissa Cole: Product perspective: ok with a 48h freeze as long as we keep the “break glass” path for real regressions. Also can we use this time to align what canary gates should block? Right now it’s unclear if warning blocks rollout or only paging.

Connor O'Brien: Agree. During freeze, I’ll draft a simple gating rule for Phase 1: warning does NOT auto-block, sustained paging DOES block + triggers rollback recommendation. We’ll iterate.

Talia Benson: Can someone state the exact start/end times and what’s included? I’ll update the release calendar + send a note.

Connor O'Brien: Start: now (post this thread). End: 48h from now (or earlier if Noah/Sean sign off that canary is stable). Included:
- Runtime build rollouts (any region/tier)
- Kernel changes / kernel selector behavior changes
- Scheduler config/flags that impact batching/queueing
- Fleet perf-impacting changes (driver, GPU SKU swap) unless urgent
Excluded:
- Telemetry pipeline fixes (w/ review)
- Console/dashboard updates

ReleaseBot: :rotating_light: Release freeze marker added to calendar: “Runtime Perf Canary Stabilization Freeze” (owner: Connor O'Brien).

Noah Patel: Plan for unfreeze criteria:
1) Min-samples + dampening config deployed
2) At least 24h without false positive warnings on the initial canary model set in us-east
3) Telemetry ingestion lag <2m p95 (Logan owns)

Sean Gallagher: +1. Also add: if we do get a real regression during freeze, we can still rollback/disable risky flags. Freeze is about forward rollouts.

Vanessa Ortiz: Confirmed: eu-west driver patch moved to Friday. I’ll note it on the infra change log.

Logan Wright: I’ll push telemetry lag fix today. Will post before/after graphs in #eng-platform.

Allison Grant: Please ensure canary alert payloads include “data freshness” / ingestion delay so oncall can quickly see when it’s a pipeline artifact.

Noah Patel: We can add a “metrics_lag_seconds” field to the incident-bot payload quickly (or at least in the runbook). I’ll sync with Caleb.

Mateo Alvarez: Approving freeze. Thanks for making this explicit—too many moving parts right now. Keep it tight (48h) and use it to get canary signal quality up.

Connor O'Brien: Thanks all. Action items:
- (Noah) deploy min-samples+dampening config today; lock canary model list for 48h
- (Logan) telemetry lag fix + report p95 lag
- (Vanessa) hold eu-west driver change until Friday
- (Connor) update release calendar + send note + draft gating rule for Phase 1

Talia Benson: I’ll send the note to eng-runtime/eng-sre/eng-platform and update the weekly release email. Please @me if anything should be exempted explicitly.

Rafael Mendes: :thumbsup: Oncall will be grateful. If we still see noise after the config changes, we should consider lowering alert sensitivity or adding a longer confirmation window before paging.

Connor O'Brien: Ack. If we’re not stable by tomorrow afternoon, we’ll extend freeze or narrow paging scope to top-3 only until we tune per-region thresholds.

source.content:
Connor O'Brien: heads up: we’re cutting the runtime-1.22 train tomorrow 10:00 PT. need final call today on what makes it vs backport. main theme is tiered batching defaults (fast/standard/cost-efficient) work.

Connor O'Brien: current candidates (runtime repo):
- pr-28431 add tiered batching profile schema
- pr-28445 scheduler uses streaming + length buckets
- pr-28458 fast tier admission control + wait budget
- pr-28466 cost-efficient aggressive batching guardrails
- pr-28473 reason codes for profile selection
- pr-28480 feature flag + kill switch
- pr-28492 runtime metrics tier labels + cardinality controls
- pr-28503 Dedicated override precedence for batching
- pr-28515 rollout automation + safeguard checks

Connor O'Brien: plus follow-ons in other repos (perf-canary, observability-pack, sdk/docs) are NOT gating the runtime cut, but we should align.

Sofia Ivanova: from scheduling side: must-have for 1.22 if we want to canary tiered defaults at all:
- 28431 (schema)
- 28445 (signals)
- 28480 (flag/kill)
- 28458 (fast tier p99 protection)

Sofia Ivanova: 28466 strongly recommended too or cost-efficient will melt queues under bursts. I’d be nervous shipping tier selection w/o it.

Mateo Alvarez: agree w/ Sofia. if we cut 1.22 without 28458 we’ll be playing whack-a-mole on fast tier tail. that’s a no.

Rafael Mendes: +1. SRE asks for: kill switch + clear overload behavior. 28480 is non-negotiable.

Allison Grant: on metrics: 28492 is *needed* for visibility + later rollout automation. but I want to be clear: tier labels increase cardinality risk. does 28492 include the guardrails we discussed (sampling + allowlist on tags)?

Julian Moore: yeah 28492 has the label allowlist + “tier” is limited to {fast,standard,cost_efficient} only. also reduced dimensions on high-volume spans (we dropped request_id, kept model_family + region).

Allison Grant: ok. if 28492 lands, I’m comfortable as long as we keep the default dashboards from exploding. (observability-pack update can follow.)

Sahana Iyer: perf perspective: 28445+28458 changed scheduling behavior enough that we need updated perf baselines. but I don’t want to block the train cut if we commit to running perf-canary on the release branch before enabling flags.

Connor O'Brien: ack. plan would be: cut 1.22 tomorrow, then perf validation on `release/runtime-1.22` branch as a gate to enabling in hosted canary.

Benji Okafor: question on 28515 (rollout automation): it’s mostly integration hooks for release tooling + some safety checks. not needed to *ship* runtime 1.22, but helpful for staged rollout so we don’t do everything manually.

Rafael Mendes: from ops: I’d like 28515 in 1.22 if it’s stable. reduces human error on expand/rollback.

Ethan Park: 28503 Dedicated override precedence is safe but it touches config evaluation. if we’re tight on timeline, we can backport it later + keep Dedicated opt-in behind control plane policy.

Chloe Martin: +1 on not blocking train on 28503. Console override UX isn’t ready until next sprint anyway.

Lena Fischer: 28503 also fixes a real precedence edge case we saw in staging (customer override not winning in one path). but if it’s risky to merge late, better to exclude and explicitly say Dedicated overrides require control plane patch.

Connor O'Brien: so: 
A) “hosted canary capable” set
B) “Dedicated opt-in ready” set

Connor O'Brien: for A, I’m hearing: 28431, 28445, 28458, 28466, 28473, 28480, 28492.

Mateo Alvarez: do we need 28473 (reason codes) for hosted canary? it’s super useful but could it slip?

Rafael Mendes: for canary triage, yes please. without reason codes, we’re guessing why profile changed (override vs overload mode vs model constraint). I’d rather keep it.

Allison Grant: +1. and it’s low risk; it’s instrumentation + structured event.

Sofia Ivanova: 28473 also helps validate selection logic quickly. we’ll save time.

Connor O'Brien: ok 28473 stays in A.

Connor O'Brien: for B (Dedicated opt-in), does 28503 need to be in the runtime train? or can it be separate patch release 1.22.1?

Ethan Park: prefer 1.22.1 for 28503. less blast radius during initial hosted rollout.

Rafael Mendes: +1. we can keep Dedicated opt-in as “flag only” and tell CS/Solutions to avoid overrides until 1.22.1.

Lena Fischer: ok but please document that. otherwise support tickets will be “override not taking effect” day 1.

Connor O'Brien: noted. I’ll add to launch brief + release notes.

Sahana Iyer: any late changes expected for 28458? last time we had a p99 regression on one model family.

Marcus Reed: 28458 includes the anti-starvation tweak + tighter wait budget. I have one more commit in review to clamp max-prefill concurrency in fast tier when queue depth spikes.

Sofia Ivanova: is that already in the PR?

Marcus Reed: it’s pushed, tests green. would like to merge tonight.

Connor O'Brien: ok, but please no new PRs today. merge into existing ones only.

ReleaseBot: PR #28458 status changed: Review approved by @sofia-ivanova. CI: running.

Allison Grant: about 28492: can we confirm we’re not tagging raw model name? need model_family only.

Julian Moore: yep. model_family only. raw model is behind a debug flag (off by default) and sampled 0.1%.

Allison Grant: great.

Connor O'Brien: timeline proposal:
- T+0 (train cut tomorrow 10:00 PT): runtime 1.22 branch created
- T+1 day: perf-canary + benchmark-lab run on release branch for top 5 model families, fast/standard tiers
- T+2 days: hosted canary enablement 1% traffic in us-east + eu-west (fast tier only)
- T+4 days: expand hosted to 10% all regions; add standard tier
- following week: cost-efficient tier opt-in (hosted), after queue depth + error budget stable
- Dedicated: opt-in behind control plane flag; overrides in 1.22.1 w/ 28503

Rafael Mendes: for canary abort criteria, we’re using: fast tier p99 +10% vs baseline for 30m OR elevated 429/load shed rate. (we’ll link the runbook.)

Sahana Iyer: and perf-canary gate is: fast p99, standard p95 within thresholds. I’ll post the baseline run IDs once done.

Mateo Alvarez: make sure we’re not promising “cost-efficient” in release notes before it’s actually available. message it as “runtime supports tiered defaults; staged rollout starting w/ fast/standard.”

Connor O'Brien: yup.

Connor O'Brien: decision checkpoint: any objections to making A set mandatory for 1.22? (28431/45/58/66/73/80/92)

Chloe Martin: no objection.

Benji Okafor: none.

Allison Grant: good.

Rafael Mendes: good. :thumbsup:

Sofia Ivanova: good.

Connor O'Brien: and B items (28503, 28515) -> target 1.22.1 unless they’re merged by EOD w/ no risk.

Benji Okafor: 28515 is small; I can land today. but if it causes churn, we can revert on release branch.

Connor O'Brien: ok, if 28515 merges by 18:00 PT and CI green, we’ll include in 1.22. otherwise 1.22.1.

ReleaseBot: PR #28466 merged into main by @lena-fischer.

ReleaseBot: PR #28473 merged into main by @julian-moore.

ReleaseBot: PR #28480 merged into main by @benji-okafor.

Sahana Iyer: pls don’t forget to tag the release branch for perf runs. perf infra needs a stable SHA.

Connor O'Brien: will do. I’ll post the exact SHA after cut.

Allison Grant: one more: if tier metrics cardinality spikes in canary, do we have a rollback path besides kill switch? (e.g., disable tier labels only)

Julian Moore: not in 1.22. tier labels are compiled in. but we can disable emission of the tier dimension via config (drops label, keeps aggregate). it’s in 28492.

Allison Grant: perfect.

Rafael Mendes: +1 that’s enough. kill switch for behavior + label drop for telemetry.

Mateo Alvarez: ok. ship it.

Connor O'Brien: final checklist for tonight:
- Sofia/Marcus: get 28458 merged
- Julian/Allison: sanity check 28492 label controls
- Benji: try to land 28515 by 18:00

Connor O'Brien: I’ll start release notes draft + train cut comms in #eng-runtime and #eng-sre once branch is cut.

ReleaseBot: PR #28458 merged into main by @marcus-reed.

ReleaseBot: PR #28492 merged into main by @julian-moore.

Benji Okafor: 28515 is in final review. no functional changes to scheduler, just rollout hook + safeguard checks.

Connor O'Brien: ok. if it doesn’t merge before cut, we’ll cherry-pick into 1.22.1.

ReleaseBot: PR #28515 status changed: merged into main by @benji-okafor.

Connor O'Brien: nice. 28515 makes the train.

Connor O'Brien: summary for the record:
- runtime-1.22 includes: 28431/45/58/66/73/80/92/28515
- runtime-1.22 does NOT include: 28503 (Dedicated override precedence) -> target 1.22.1
- staged rollout starts w/ hosted canary; Dedicated overrides after 1.22.1

Connor O'Brien: please reply here if anything looks off before tomorrow’s cut.

source.messages:
Connor O'Brien: hey folks — need to lock down what’s going into runtime-1.22 re: KV compaction. Trying to avoid “big risky PRs land Friday, branch cut Monday” :grimacing:

Connor O'Brien: questions:
1) are we carrying compaction in 1.22 or deferring to 1.23?
2) what’s the default state of the feature flag(s) on day-1 of release?
3) what’s our rollback story if p99 spikes after the train goes out?

Talia Benson: +1, also need a single sentence for release notes like “present but disabled by default” vs “enabled for hosted canary only”.

Omar El-Sayed: we’re planning to carry in 1.22, but *flagged*. goal is to get allocator + scheduler hooks in, default OFF globally, then progressively enable via config.

Samir Qureshi: for “what lands”: would like these PRs in 1.22:
- pr-19342 (metrics) ✅ already in main
- pr-19357 (core allocator changes + relocation primitives) behind `kv_compaction_enabled`
- pr-19371 (scheduler budget enforcement) behind same gate
- pr-19422 (kill-switch/config plumbing) so oncall can disable quickly

Samir Qureshi: eviction (pr-19388) is nice-to-have but more behavioral risk. we can keep it disabled (or not ship it) and still get fragmentation improvements via compaction alone.

Sofia Ivanova: +1 on keeping eviction separate. scheduler hook is the bigger safety net (budgeting at prefill/decode boundaries) and reduces “mystery stalls”.

Connor O'Brien: ok. my bias is: ship the plumbing + metrics + compaction core if fully gated, but avoid “partial gating” where code paths run w/o being obvious.

Benji Okafor: on flags: we can do a single top-level gate plus sub-flags.
- `kv_compaction_enabled` (master)
- `kv_compaction_budget_us` / `kv_compaction_budget_bytes`
- `kv_compaction_frag_threshold`
- `kv_eviction_enabled`

Benji Okafor: defaults in 1.22 config package can be master=OFF, eviction=OFF. budget defaults present but inert. then Hosted can flip master ON for canary cohorts via rollout config.

Sean Gallagher: from SRE perspective: we need “OFF by default” for everyone except internal canary, at least for first train. we can’t ship with “surprise ON” anywhere.

Allison Grant: also please ensure metrics emit even when disabled (or at least allocator fragmentation ratio) so we can compare before/after when we enable.

Omar El-Sayed: metrics are already unconditional (fragmentation ratio, arena stats). compaction-specific metrics (bytes moved, compaction time) will be 0 when disabled.

Rafael Mendes: on rollback story: we need kill-switch that can be flipped from control plane config w/o restart. also need documented “what to check” for oncall.

Benji Okafor: pr-19422 adds config plumbing so we can flip at runtime; propagation is ~30–60s for Hosted, longer for Dedicated depending on customer config sync. but still much better than “revert + redeploy”.

Connor O'Brien: timeline check — runtime-1.22 branch cut is next week. what’s the landing risk for pr-19357/pr-19371? those are not small.

Samir Qureshi: pr-19357 is the biggest diff. it’s behind the gate but touches allocator internals. we’ve got unit tests + stress tests in progress. would prefer to land no later than EOD tomorrow to get weekend soak.

Sofia Ivanova: pr-19371 is smaller and depends on 19357. I can land it right after 19357, but if 19357 slips, 19371 slips.

Talia Benson: can we commit to “stop landing new compaction code after branch cut” and only do backports if there’s a must-fix correctness issue?

Connor O'Brien: yes. for release stability: if these miss the cut, we don’t cram them.

Omar El-Sayed: agreed. compaction in 1.22 is “present but disabled by default”; enablement is a post-release config rollout.

Sean Gallagher: can we explicitly state: master flag OFF for Hosted + Dedicated + Private by default in 1.22 artifacts.

Benji Okafor: yep. I’ll set defaults in the runtime config bundle to OFF. rollout system will be the only thing turning it ON (and only for canary).

Allison Grant: please also add guardrail: if compaction time exceeds budget or bytes moved spikes, it should self-throttle. (I know that’s in design, just want it called out for release signoff.)

Samir Qureshi: already in implementation: budget enforced per scheduler tick; compaction does at most N micro-steps and yields.

Connor O'Brien: what about eviction? decision for 1.22: ship code but keep OFF? or exclude entirely?

Omar El-Sayed: preference: if eviction PR isn’t merged & stabilized by cut, we exclude. if it merges early enough, we can carry it but leave `kv_eviction_enabled=OFF` everywhere. either way, no runtime behavior change unless master ON.

Rafael Mendes: please don’t carry eviction if it increases risk of correctness bugs even when disabled (e.g., code paths compiled in but not executed should be safe, but we’ve been burned by “disabled” code still affecting state).

Sofia Ivanova: eviction touches selection logic + pinned sessions. even if off, the extra state tracking might exist. I’d vote exclude for this train unless we have a compelling reason.

Connor O'Brien: ok — release call: runtime-1.22 includes metrics + config plumbing + compaction core + scheduler budget hook. eviction is excluded from 1.22 unless Omar/Samir say it’s zero-risk and fully baked by tomorrow.

Talia Benson: :thumbsup: I’ll update the release plan ticket w/ that statement.

ReleaseBot: runtime-1.22 branch cut reminder: freeze begins in ~4 days. Please have high-risk PRs merged and soaked before freeze.

Connor O'Brien: action items:
- @Samir land pr-19357 by EOD tomorrow or call it out if slipping
- @Sofia follow w/ pr-19371 after
- @Benji ensure defaults OFF in 1.22 config + confirm kill-switch works w/o restart
- @Rafael + @Sean confirm rollback authority + comms (who flips kill-switch)

Sean Gallagher: SRE side: rollback authority during canary = oncall SRE can flip kill-switch, notify runtime in #eng-oncall. after-hours = same.

Rafael Mendes: +1, but runbook needs explicit “symptoms” (p99 spike + bytes_moved correlation) to avoid flapping.

Allison Grant: I’ll add a note to the metrics standard doc for “signals that indicate compaction-caused latency” (bytes_moved burst + compaction_time_us + p99).

Omar El-Sayed: thanks. will keep eviction out unless we magically finish it early + soak. otherwise target 1.22.x follow-on.

Connor O'Brien: perfect. ping this thread when 19357 is merged so I can mark the train risk as “contained by flag + kill-switch”.

source.content:
Connor O'Brien: trying to lock release targeting for the hardware tuning profiles pack. which runtime release gets it? do we put it into runtime-1.x (default on?) or ship behind flags / as external bundle first?

Talia Benson: +1 need a decision today-ish so we can align docs + rollout checklist. current milestone doc assumes "available in Hosted first" then Dedicated/Private.

Noah Patel: my preference: land schema + loader in 1.x ASAP, but keep actual tuned presets behind a feature flag + default OFF until perf-canary is green across all target pools.

Benji Okafor: loader + override order is already basically done (PR-21991 + helm wiring PR-612). the question is do we backport those to the current stable branch.

Connor O'Brien: release train: runtime-1.14 branch cut is in 6 days. runtime-1.15 cut is ~3 weeks after. if we miss 1.14, Private customers won't see it until mid-Jan.

Ethan Park: schema/validation is low risk, I'd backport that to 1.14. profile pack content is higher risk (tail latency regressions). I'd keep the tuned profiles gated.

Hiro Tanaka: kernels side: H100 tuned preset is trending good on throughput, but we still had that prefill/decode fairness tail issue earlier in the week. I don't want default-on in the first release it appears.

Sofia Ivanova: +1. scheduling knobs have a lot of "it depends". we can ship the modes (balanced vs throughput-first), but turning them on by default should happen via fleet defaults after we watch canary.

Sean Gallagher: from SRE: we need a fast kill switch per pool. if it ships in 1.14, it must be default OFF and we need the override to disable without redeploy (or at least with a config push).

Irene Choi: infra can handle pool-level config. but we need a clean precedence order: fleet default < pool override < customer override (Dedicated/Private) < env var emergency override.

Benji Okafor: precedence order is in the ADR draft; env var override is highest for emergency. loader supports "profile=disabled" + explicit profile name.

Marissa Cole: product note: console UX for rollouts expects a versioned profile pack string (e.g. hwpack-2025.12.0) and a "source" label (fleet vs pool vs customer). if we ship in 1.14 we should include the metrics/labels or else oncall will have a bad time.

Quentin Harris: compatibility matrix logging is in PR-22041 (startup logs). if we’re backporting to 1.14, we should include that too, otherwise Private deployments will be confusing when they pin old runtime but drop in a new pack.

Kira Volkov: perf-canary gates won’t be ready for 1.14 branch cut. but that's ok if default OFF. we can enable in Hosted canary pools via config once gates are green.

Connor O'Brien: propose:
1) runtime-1.14: backport schema + validation + loader + override precedence + metrics/logs. ship with "profiles pack" embedded but not enabled by default. B200 stays behind experimental flag.
2) runtime-1.15: consider flipping H100/L40S defaults ON in Hosted-only after 1-2 weeks of canary, but keep Dedicated/Private opt-in until we have more data.
Thoughts?

Hiro Tanaka: ok with 1.14 backport if we keep pack content minimal: H100 balanced + L40S memory guardrails only. no aggressive throughput-first defaults.

Sofia Ivanova: agree. plus put throughput-first behind a separate flag (`RUNTIME_SCHED_PRESET=throughput_first`) so we don't conflate with hw profile enablement.

Ethan Park: we should be explicit in release notes: "available" != "enabled". wording matters. also: if pack is embedded, we need a way to hotfix pack content without patching runtime.

Benji Okafor: embedded pack makes hotfix harder. ADR leaned toward external bundle as well. but external bundle has rollout complexity for Hosted (need artifact distribution).

Irene Choi: for Hosted, we can mount an external config bundle per pool pretty easily (we already do for tokenizer assets). for Dedicated/Private, Helm values can point to a configmap/secret.

Connor O'Brien: ok so maybe:
- runtime ships loader + schema + baseline fallback
- pack ships as separate artifact (hwpack) that can be updated independently
- runtime has a "built-in default pack" only as a last resort

Noah Patel: +1. reduces "runtime patch for config tweak" churn. also helps B200: we can yank/replace the B200 profile in a pack version without forcing a runtime upgrade.

Sean Gallagher: SRE strongly prefers separate artifact + config switch. if we hit a regression at 2am, swapping pack version or disabling is way easier than rolling runtime.

Marissa Cole: ok but then the console needs to show both runtime version and pack version. fine, but we should scope it.

Quentin Harris: and compatibility checks should validate (runtime semver) x (pack semver). if pack is external, startup should warn and fall back to "disabled" if incompatible.

Benji Okafor: loader already does "fail closed": if it can’t validate pack schema or compat range, it logs + selects safe defaults (equivalent to profile disabled). metrics tag `profile_select_reason` covers it.

DeployBot: Release calendar reminder: runtime-1.14 branch cut scheduled for 2025-12-02 18:00 PT. Please have backport list in #eng-releases by EOD 2025-11-30.

Talia Benson: can someone summarize decision + what’s included in 1.14? I need to update the release checklist + docs ticket.

Connor O'Brien: summary proposal (unless strong objections):
- Target runtime-1.14 for: schema/validation, loader + override order, compatibility logging, selection metrics/debug hooks.
- Do NOT enable any hardware profiles by default in 1.14.
- Ship the tuned profiles as an external "hwpack" artifact v2025.12.0; Hosted canary will pin pack version and enable per pool.
- B200 profile remains experimental + default OFF; enabling requires both `enable_hw_profiles=true` AND `enable_b200_experimental=true`.
- Backport policy: 1.14 gets infra plumbing + safety; pack updates iterate out-of-band; only critical loader bugs get patch releases.

Hiro Tanaka: :thumbsup: from kernels.

Sofia Ivanova: :thumbsup: scheduling.

Sean Gallagher: :thumbsup: SRE. add explicit rollback note: "set profile=disabled" at pool level, and/or roll back pack version.

Irene Choi: :thumbsup: infra. will start wiring pool config to accept `hwpack_version` + `hw_profile_enable`.

Ethan Park: :thumbsup: will update schema doc to clarify "pack is external" and compat ranges.

Marissa Cole: ok. I’ll sync with Elliot/Kira Thompson on wording so we don’t imply SLA guarantees.

Quentin Harris: I’ll add a small checklist item for Private: runtime upgrade required for loader, but pack can be updated independently once loader is present.

Connor O'Brien: cool. I’ll post backport list in this channel and open the cherry-pick requests. if anyone has must-have PRs for 1.14, reply with links today.

Kira Volkov: adding note: perf-canary gates will key off pack version, not runtime version, so we can test pack changes without rebuilding runtime. I'll coordinate with Aisha on dashboards.

Talia Benson: thanks. updating ENG-4830 + release checklist doc now.

source.text:
Connor O'Brien: kicking off thread for Optimize quality gates beta rollout. goal: internal beta -> limited prod, with release notes + change mgmt. need owners + dates so I can book release train slots.

Talia Benson: thx. what’s target window? last I saw was “late Jan staging enforcement / Feb limited prod”. still true?

Aditya Rao: yep. internal beta: enforce in staging + a couple internal routes w/ flags week of Jan 20. limited prod for hosted API subset starting Feb 10-ish (need SRE signoff).

Sahana Iyer: from release/CI side: we’ll need a “required checks” list for anything touching gating codepaths. also need a plan for GPU scarcity for eval jobs during the beta.

Paula Mendes: rollout controller piece is in flight. release impact: canary promotion will hard-block on gate failures. we should call that out in release notes + change template.

Connor O'Brien: cool. here’s what I think we need before beta starts:
- release notes draft (internal + limited customer)
- change request template filled (owner/approver)
- runbook link + oncall prep checklist
- feature flags + rollback plan validated
- comms plan (#eng, #support, #sales)
pls confirm / add.

Sean Gallagher: add SLO + paging expectations. we don’t want oncall getting paged for flaky evals day 1. at least “alerts in ticket mode” for first week.

Allison Grant: +1. observability spec says we can start with dashboards + warning alerts, then enable paging once flake rate stable. release notes should mention “gate execution latency” can block rollouts.

Dylan Brooks: Console UI will show “Blocked by quality gate” w/ reasons + artifact links. we need copy review + support enablement because customers will ask “why can’t I apply this optimization?”

Chloe Martin: we’ll ship Console API changes behind a flag too. need to coordinate to avoid UI calling fields that aren’t there yet.

Elliot Price: I can own docs/release notes wording. question: do we want separate notes for (a) internal beta and (b) limited prod? or single doc w/ sections.

Connor O'Brien: separate. internal beta note can be more candid (“known limitations”), limited prod needs customer-safe wording + support FAQ.

ReleaseBot: Reminder: Production changes require a Change Record at least 48h in advance. Template: https://go.redwood/changes/template (include rollback + monitoring).

Talia Benson: I’ll coordinate the change record + approvals. Aditya/Paula can you give me the “go/no-go” criteria for limited prod (pass rate, flake rate, runtime bounds)?

Aditya Rao: draft criteria we discussed in #product:
- gate pass rate on internal workloads >= 90% for smoke tier; full tier pass >= 80% (expect some real fails)
- flake rate <= 5% (after reruns policy)
- p95 gate end-to-end runtime <= 25 min for full tier, <= 6 min smoke
- no P0/P1 incidents from enforcement in staging

Sean Gallagher: add: “no sustained increase in rollout pipeline timeouts” + “eval-harness queue depth within normal range”. otherwise we’ll just DDOS ourselves w/ gating.

Sahana Iyer: on GPU scarcity: we should cap concurrent gate runs per region + have a backoff. in CI, we can run a stubbed integration test suite, but the real GPU eval jobs should be nightly / pre-merge only for specific labels.

Connor O'Brien: for beta, how do we want the release mechanics?
- deploy gating enforcement service changes
- deploy eval-harness trigger endpoint
- enable flags per route
- enable rollout controller block
need ordering.

Paula Mendes: ordering suggestion:
1) ship everything dark (no enforcement)
2) enable gating runs (record-only) to validate pipeline + artifacts
3) enable enforcement at “apply” boundary in staging
4) enable rollout controller block in staging
5) internal routes in prod w/ smoke-tier only
6) expand to limited customer routes w/ smoke->full

Allison Grant: and between 2) and 3) we need dashboards live + alert rules in “no-page” mode. otherwise we’ll be blind.

Leila Farouk: QA note: we should do at least one end-to-end rehearsal with a forced fail + override + rollback. support needs screenshots + expected states.

Dylan Brooks: override UX: console shows “Override requested” -> “Override granted (expires …)”. we should include the approval group name in the UI so people know who to ping.

Talia Benson: who is approver group for beta? last email thread said tighten after near-miss.

Connor O'Brien: per latest: for limited prod, overrides require (a) Optimize PM (Aditya) + (b) Eng oncall manager or SRE delegate. internal beta can be just PM + eng lead. confirm?

Sean Gallagher: for prod, I want SRE in the loop. “SRE oncall + Optimize PM” is fine. also require expiration <= 7 days.

Aditya Rao: works. we’ll document “max 7 days, must attach evidence + follow-up task”.

Elliot Price: release notes draft outline:
- What’s new: quality gates required before Optimize config changes (batching/caching/quant)
- Impact: can block apply + can block canary promotion
- How it works: smoke tier then full tier; artifacts + deltas; reruns on flake
- Failure modes: eval timeouts, flake, true regression
- What to do: view artifacts; rerun; request override; rollback suggestion
- Known limitations (beta): limited prompt set coverage, region capacity, private deployments manual
- Contacts: #help, #eng-ml, oncall rotation
ok?

Chloe Martin: add “API behavior changes”: apply endpoint returns structured failure reason + gate_run_id. support/devs will see new error codes.

Sahana Iyer: also call out the CI requirement: any PR touching gating must pass schema validation + integration contract tests. devs will ask why merges are blocked.

Connor O'Brien: change management steps:
- Talia files change record for staging enforcement (Jan 20) + prod limited (Feb 10)
- Elliot writes internal beta release note by Jan 15; customer-safe note by Feb 1
- SRE signs off on alerts/dashboards readiness by Jan 18
- rehearsal game day Jan 22 (Leila + Dylan + SRE)
- comms: post to #eng and #support 1 week ahead
sound right?

Talia Benson: I’ll put those in a tracker doc + send calendar holds. Connor can you be DRI for the Jan 22 game day scheduling?

Connor O'Brien: yep.

Allison Grant: last thing: add a “rollback switch” runbook step. if gates start timing out, we need to disable enforcement without redeploy. ensure flag is at runtime + rollout controller level.

Paula Mendes: rollout controller already has a kill switch flag. will document.

Sean Gallagher: + confirm we won’t page on “single gate fail” (that’s expected). paging should be on “systemic failure: gate infra down, timeouts spike, pass rate cliff across many routes”.

Connor O'Brien: ok. I’ll summarize action items:
- Elliot: internal beta notes (Jan 15), limited prod notes (Feb 1)
- Talia: change records + approvals + comms plan
- Allison/Sean: dashboards/alerts ready + paging policy
- Paula: rollout controller ordering + kill switch doc
- Sahana: CI guidance + capacity caps for eval jobs
- Leila/Dylan/Chloe: Jan 22 rehearsal + support-ready artifacts/error codes
reply if anything missing.

Aditya Rao: looks good. I’ll also share go/no-go criteria in the change record so approvals have something concrete.

ReleaseBot: Change Record tip: include “customer impact assessment” and “support readiness” checkboxes. https://go.redwood/changes/checklist

Connor O'Brien: thx all. I’ll follow up w/ links once docs exist.

source.content:
Talia Benson: GA enablement window for hot-route capacity protection starting now (per release train). Goal: flip flags globally (Hosted API + Dedicated) w/ enterprise route protections.

Checklist for go/no-go:
- us-east + eu-west canaries clean (allowlist)
- SRE dashboards green (enterprise p95/p99 + 5xx + shed rate)
- no metric cardinality regressions
- rollback plan confirmed

Links:
- Dashboard: https://grafana.redwood.ai/d/hosted-hot-routes-ga
- Runbook: https://confluence.redwood.ai/display/SRE/runbook-overload-protection-and-admission-control
- Launch checklist: https://drive.google.com/redwood/launch-checklist-hot-route-capacity-protection

Benji Okafor: Flags queued for stepwise rollout:
1) `hot_route_protection.gateway_enforce=true`
2) `hot_route_protection.routing_admission=true`
3) `hot_route_protection.fallback_guardrails=true`

Order: us-east -> eu-west -> us-west. 10 min soak between steps, 30 min between regions.

deploy-bot: Starting rollout job `rrt-flagflip-5271` (env=prod)
- region=us-east
- flags=hot_route_protection.gateway_enforce,hot_route_protection.routing_admission,hot_route_protection.fallback_guardrails
- allowlist=acme-ai,proxima-bank (enterprise)

Amara Diallo: Gateway side looks good in canary since yesterday. 429 schema is stable + headers present.
Quick reminder: if we see customers complaining about “random 429s”, check `x-redwood-throttle-reason` first.
Expected values: `quota_exceeded`, `rate_limited`, `overload_shed`.

Sergio Costa: Looking at dashboard now. us-east canary last 30m:
- enterprise chat p95: 480-520ms (flat)
- enterprise 5xx: 0.02%
- best-effort shed rate: spikes to ~6% during the top-of-hour burst (expected)
- queue depth: elevated but stable
No burn alerts firing.

Rafael Mendes: :thumbsup: SRE perspective: canary behaves like game day. Shedding is happening early on best-effort, enterprise stays within SLO.

Connor O'Brien: RC signed bundle validation thread for private-2026.01-rc2. Goal: confirm the *same* bundle verifies + installs across staging envs (usw2/euc1/gov-stg) and capture any installer version gotchas before we bless the tag.

release-bot: Build published: `private-2026.01-rc2` (build_id: `pvt-202601rc2+7f3c2c1`). Offline bundle: `redwood-private-offline-bundle-2026.01-rc2.tgz` (sha256: `c7b6…9d2e`). Signature: `manifest.sig` (detached). Manifest: `manifest.json`.

Sahana Iyer: I pulled from artifacts bucket and did local verify on build runner.

Sahana Iyer: Commands:
```
sha256sum redwood-private-offline-bundle-2026.01-rc2.tgz
# c7b6...9d2e  redwood-private-offline-bundle-2026.01-rc2.tgz

tar -tzf redwood-private-offline-bundle-2026.01-rc2.tgz | head
# manifest.json
# manifest.sig
# images/index.json
# charts/redwood-platform-1.18.0.tgz
# ...

rpi bundle inspect ./redwood-private-offline-bundle-2026.01-rc2.tgz --verify
# ✅ signature valid
# ✅ manifest canonicalization ok
# ✅ all artifact hashes match
# signing key: RI-OFFLINE-2025Q4-01 (kid: 3d:91:...)
```
Looks clean on my side.

Ethan Park: Ran installer preflight against staging-usw2 airgap sim (no outbound). Installer versions matter here. On 1.9.1, it fails with unknown key.

Ethan Park: 1.9.1 output:
```
ERROR [BNDL-VER-004] Untrusted signing key
  key_id=3d91...
  bundle=redwood-private-offline-bundle-2026.01-rc2.tgz
Remediation: upgrade installer to >= 1.9.3 or update trust store (trusted_keys.json)
```
On 1.9.3 it passes.

Connor O'Brien: Thanks. That matches expectation (trust store update landed in 1.9.3). Can you confirm exact installer build you used?

Ethan Park: Yep:
- `rpi version` = `1.9.3+installer.24859`
- bundle = `2026.01-rc2`
- preflight cmd:
```
rpi install preflight --air-gapped --bundle ./redwood-private-offline-bundle-2026.01-rc2.tgz
```

Jordan Lee: +1, we should call out in release notes that *bundles are signed with the new root* and older installers will treat it as unknown key. Not a regression, but it will look like one to customers.

Kira Thompson: I can add a blunt line in the draft release notes + docs: “Installer >= 1.9.3 required for 2026.01 signed bundles”. Also add the error code + screenshot.

Devon Price: FYI gov-stg cluster was using 1.9.2 (oops). Same untrusted key failure. After updating to 1.9.3, preflight and install went through.

Devon Price: install notes (gov-stg):
- preflight ok
- helm apply ok
- pod start ok
- console shows build_id label `pvt-202601rc2+7f3c2c1` (chart annotations from pr-18335 are present)

Sean Gallagher: Any time-skew issues with cert validity window? we had that “not yet valid” thing on pilots.

Ethan Park: I tried with the staging airgap VM clock skewed +18h and got the expected fail:
```
ERROR [BNDL-VER-006] Signature not yet valid
  now=2026-01-xxT...
  valid_from=...
Remediation: fix system time (NTP/manual) or rerun with --time-override (diagnostics only)
```
So the UX path is working, and it’s still fail-closed unless you use the override.

Neha Kapoor: Good. For RC validation, we should capture that we’re not requiring outbound time source and we’re not silently ignoring it. Also: did anyone try the break-glass flag? we need to confirm it logs loudly.

Logan Wright: I tested break-glass on staging-usw2 (policy gate enabled).

Logan Wright: Output:
```
WARN Verification override enabled (--break-glass)
AUDIT bundle_verification_override_used reason="staging validation" operator="logan.wright" bundle_build_id="pvt-202601rc2+7f3c2c1"
```
Installer still writes the local audit event file under `/var/log/redwood-private/audit.log`.

Connor O'Brien: Great. We’ll keep break-glass documented as support-only, not in the normal happy path.

Sahana Iyer: One more thing: chart tarball checksums in manifest look aligned. I verified `charts/redwood-platform-1.18.0.tgz` sha matches manifest entry. No mismatch like we saw last week.

Jordan Lee: Confirmed canonicalization hash is stable across re-pack. I re-tarred with deterministic flags and signature validation still holds (manifest signature is detached and over canonical JSON, not tar ordering).

Connor O'Brien: Perfect. Any failures left?

Devon Price: Only “gotcha” is installer version floor. Otherwise rc2 seems good.

Kira Thompson: Drafting doc snippet now. Proposed wording:
- “Signed offline bundles require Redwood Private Installer v1.9.3+.”
- “If you see BNDL-VER-004 Untrusted signing key, update installer trust store / upgrade installer.”
I’ll link to internal troubleshooting page.

Sean Gallagher: From SRE POV, we’re good to proceed as long as release gating checks that the installer artifact in the release bundle is >= 1.9.3. Otherwise we’ll ship a bundle that can’t install itself.

Connor O'Brien: +1. Action items:
- Connor: update release gating checklist to enforce installer >= 1.9.3 for 2026.01 train.
- Kira: add installer version note + error code reference to release notes/docs.
- Ethan/Devon: attach preflight logs from usw2/euc1/gov-stg to the release ticket.
If no new issues by EOD, I’ll mark RC validated and move to cut candidate.

release-bot: Reminder: RC sign-off pending. Required approvals: Release Eng + Security Eng + SRE.

Neha Kapoor: SRE sign-off: ok from me based on above results. Just don’t skip the gating update.

Connor O'Brien: :thumbsup: Thanks all. Marking this thread as the RC validation record for rc2.

source.text:
Connor O'Brien: putting together the gating checklist for Private releases now that signed offline bundles are required. can folks drop MUST-HAVEs + who signs off? goal is “no cut unless all green” (ties to ENG-4862).

Sahana Iyer: build side: 
- ✅ bundle build is deterministic (same inputs -> same manifest hash) + build logs archived
- ✅ manifest canonicalization step enforced (no pretty-print drift)
- ✅ hashes computed for *every* artifact in bundle (installer, helm chart tgz, image index list, runtime/model artifacts, sbom)
- ✅ CI attestation/provenance generated + stored with release artifacts
sign-off: Build/CI (me) + Release Eng.

Ethan Park: installer gating items:
- ✅ installer preflight verifies signature (fail-closed) on RC bundle
- ✅ `offline-bundle inspect` command returns PASS on RC bundle and prints expected versions/build-id
- ✅ downgrade prevention check passes (signed version metadata + local state record)
- ✅ air-gapped mode confirmed: installer doesn’t try to fetch anything outbound when bundle has all artifacts
sign-off: Installer eng (me) + Release Eng.

Marcus Lin: security gating:
- ✅ signing key used is from protected store (HSM-backed in prod) + operator approvals recorded
- ✅ signature envelope matches ADR-0147 (alg + detached signature layout) and verifier accepts ONLY approved algorithms
- ✅ trust store contains current + next key (if rotation window) and unknown key = hard fail
- ✅ tamper tests pass (modified manifest, swapped artifact, truncated bundle) — installer blocks
- ✅ break-glass flag is policy-gated + emits explicit audit warning (and docs point to exception process)
sign-off: Security Eng (Marcus) for crypto/verification + Security leadership sponsor if any exception.

Olga Petrov: GRC/compliance gating:
- ✅ key ceremony evidence captured for the key currently active (roles, approvals, audit artifacts)
- ✅ evidence pack includes: signing run logs, artifact inventory, checksums, who approved, where keys live, retention policy
- ✅ release notes + customer-facing docs updated w/ required installer version + verification steps
sign-off: GRC (me) + (Aisha if we’re doing anything non-standard/exception).

Vivek Kulkarni: product gating (Private):
- ✅ GA criteria check for this release stage (pilot/limited/GA) is met per PM-894
- ✅ comms plan ready: what changes for existing Private customers (installer version requirements + trust store updates)
- ✅ known limitations documented + support path clear
sign-off: Product (me).

Neha Kapoor: SRE/ops gating:
- ✅ signing pipeline health checks green (no flakes) + metrics dashboard not red (ENG-4874)
- ✅ on-call runbooks exist for signing pipeline failures + key rotation emergency + verification failure triage
- ✅ release rollback plan confirmed (what we do if bad bundle published)
sign-off: SRE exec sponsor (me) + Sean for runbook readiness.

Sean Gallagher: adding: 
- ✅ staging validation: verify + install path executed in a “no egress” environment (simulated air-gap) on RC
- ✅ alert routes configured for signing stage failures + verifier error spikes (where applicable)
- ✅ support bundle includes signed bundle metadata + verification status (helps triage)
sign-off: SRE (me).

Dev Patel: support readiness gating:
- ✅ runbook published: “offline bundle verification failures” w/ error code taxonomy + remediation
- ✅ FAQ/common errors doc updated (hash mismatch after transfer, unknown key after rotation, time skew)
- ✅ escalation tree: Support -> Release Eng/Security Eng/SRE defined + on-call contacts for release week
sign-off: Support readiness (me).

Logan Wright: telemetry/audit gating:
- ✅ verification audit events emitted locally (success/failure + reason codes) w/ redaction config default sane
- ✅ exporter schema updated for customers w/ SIEM forwarding
sign-off: Telemetry/audit owner (me) + Kevin (not in thread) for schema sanity.

Hanae Suzuki: key management gating:
- ✅ trust store update mechanism validated in air-gapped flow (customer can add new pubkey file offline)
- ✅ rotation drill completed or explicitly waived w/ documented risk
sign-off: KMS/secrets (me) + Release Eng.

Daniel Carter: evidence detail: pls ensure we store a single “release evidence index” doc that links to:
- signing job run id
- bundle build id
- manifest sha256
- signature file sha256
- SBOM hash
- approver list + timestamps
makes questionnaires way faster.

ReleaseBot: Reminder: Private RC cut window in 48h. If gating checklist not finalized, notify #eng-releases and update the cut plan.

Connor O'Brien: this is great. i’m going to consolidate into a single checklist we can paste into the release cut issue + the RC thread. draft below — reply w/ edits.

Connor O'Brien: ✅ **Private Release Gating Checklist — Signed Offline Bundles** (must be green before we cut/publish Private artifacts)

1) **Bundle contents + manifest correctness** (Owner: Build/CI)
- [ ] Offline bundle produced by release pipeline (no manual bundling)
- [ ] Manifest present + canonicalized (stable ordering/encoding)
- [ ] Hashes present for all required artifacts (charts, images index, installer, runtime/model artifacts, SBOM)
- [ ] SBOM pointer + SBOM hash included
Sign-off: Sahana Iyer + Connor O’Brien

2) **Signing + key custody** (Owner: Release Eng + Sec)
- [ ] Bundle/manifest signed with approved key from protected store
- [ ] Approvals recorded (signing operator + required reviewer)
- [ ] Signature format matches ADR-0147 (detached `manifest.sig` layout, etc.)
- [ ] Key ceremony evidence is current + linked in evidence index
Sign-off: Connor O’Brien + Marcus Lin (+ Olga for evidence completeness)

3) **Verification in installer (fail closed)** (Owner: Installer)
- [ ] Preflight verifies signature and rejects mismatch/unknown key
- [ ] `offline-bundle inspect` shows PASS on RC bundle and prints build-id/versions
- [ ] Downgrade protection enforced via signed version metadata
- [ ] Air-gapped mode: no outbound fetch attempts
Sign-off: Ethan Park + Connor O’Brien

4) **Test suite + tamper coverage** (Owner: Eng)
- [ ] Integration tests pass: tampered manifest, swapped artifact, truncated bundle, replay/downgrade, time-skew scenario
- [ ] RC validated in staging “no egress” environment
Sign-off: Ethan Park + Sean Gallagher

5) **Trust store + rotation readiness** (Owner: KMS/Release)
- [ ] Trust store contains active key(s) + supports rollover
- [ ] Rotation comms/doc path validated (customer can update trust roots offline)
Sign-off: Hanae Suzuki + Connor O’Brien

6) **Audit/telemetry + export schema** (Owner: Telemetry)
- [ ] Local audit events emitted for verify success/failure w/ reason code taxonomy
- [ ] Exporter mappings updated (if enabled) + redaction defaults reviewed
Sign-off: Logan Wright (+ Kevin Osei async)

7) **Docs + release notes + support readiness** (Owner: Docs/Support/Product)
- [ ] Customer-facing docs updated (verification steps, required installer version)
- [ ] Release notes entry prepared (migration notes + known limitations)
- [ ] Support runbooks + FAQ updated; escalation paths confirmed
Sign-off: Vivek Kulkarni + Dev Patel (+ Docs owner async)

8) **Compliance evidence pack** (Owner: GRC)
- [ ] Evidence index doc created + links to signing logs, manifest/signature hashes, approvers, retention location
Sign-off: Olga Petrov (+ Daniel Carter)

9) **Exception/break-glass** (Only if needed)
- [ ] If break-glass used, approval captured + customer comms + explicit audit markers
Sign-off: Marcus Lin + Dr. Aisha Rahman + Connor O’Brien

If all sign-offs collected -> ✅ RC can be promoted / Private artifacts published.

Connor O'Brien: if we agree, i’ll paste into the cut checklist + pin it.

Marcus Lin: looks good. add explicit “unknown signing key = hard fail unless break-glass path” in section 3 or 5. we had debate on this and want it unambiguous.

Sahana Iyer: +1. also suggest adding “manifest sha256 recorded in evidence index” under section 1/8 (we always end up chasing it later).

Dev Patel: can we add links placeholders like “runbook-offline-bundle-verification-failures” so folks don’t hunt? i’ll DM you the final confluence URLs once doc publish finishes.

Sean Gallagher: minor: under staging validation, call out we should test on *the minimum supported installer version* too (customers lag).

Vivek Kulkarni: +1 to Sean. also include “required installer version” in release notes gating explicitly.

Connor O'Brien: edits in. 
- added unknown key hard fail note 
- added manifest sha capture 
- added min supported installer version check
will pin the final checklist in the cut thread + copy to the RC validation thread. thanks all :thumbsup:

ReleaseBot: Pinned by Connor O’Brien: “Private Release Gating Checklist — Signed Offline Bundles”

- ✅ downgrade prevention check passes (signed version metadata + local state record)
- ✅ air-gapped mode confirmed: installer doesn’t try to fetch anything outbound when bundle has all artifacts
sign-off: Installer eng (me) + Release Eng.

Marcus Lin: security gating:
- ✅ signing key used is from protected store (HSM-backed in prod) + operator approvals recorded
- ✅ signature envelope matches ADR-0147 (alg + detached signature layout) and verifier accepts ONLY approved algorithms
- ✅ trust store contains current + next key (if rotation window) and unknown key = hard fail
- ✅ tamper tests pass (modified manifest, swapped artifact, truncated bundle) — installer blocks
- ✅ break-glass flag is policy-gated + emits explicit audit warning (and docs point to exception process)
sign-off: Security Eng (Marcus) for crypto/verification + Security leadership sponsor if any exception.

Olga Petrov: GRC/compliance gating:
- ✅ key ceremony evidence captured for the key currently active (roles, approvals, audit artifacts)
- ✅ evidence pack includes: signing run logs, artifact inventory, checksums, who approved, where keys live, retention policy
- ✅ release notes + customer-facing docs updated w/ required installer version + verification steps
sign-off: GRC (me) + (Aisha if we’re doing anything non-standard/exception).

Vivek Kulkarni: product gating (Private):
- ✅ GA criteria check for this release stage (pilot/limited/GA) is met per PM-894
- ✅ comms plan ready: what changes for existing Private customers (installer version requirements + trust store updates)
- ✅ known limitations documented + support path clear
sign-off: Product (me).

Neha Kapoor: SRE/ops gating:
- ✅ signing pipeline health checks green (no flakes) + metrics dashboard not red (ENG-4874)
- ✅ on-call runbooks exist for signing pipeline failures + key rotation emergency + verification failure triage
- ✅ release rollback plan confirmed (what we do if bad bundle published)
sign-off: SRE exec sponsor (me) + Sean for runbook readiness.

Sean Gallagher: adding: 
- ✅ staging validation: verify + install path executed in a “no egress” environment (simulated air-gap) on RC
- ✅ alert routes configured for signing stage failures + verifier error spikes (where applicable)
- ✅ support bundle includes signed bundle metadata + verification status (helps triage)
sign-off: SRE (me).

Dev Patel: support readiness gating:
- ✅ runbook published: “offline bundle verification failures” w/ error code taxonomy + remediation
- ✅ FAQ/common errors doc updated (hash mismatch after transfer, unknown key after rotation, time skew)
- ✅ escalation tree: Support -> Release Eng/Security Eng/SRE defined + on-call contacts for release week
sign-off: Support readiness (me).

Logan Wright: telemetry/audit gating:
- ✅ verification audit events emitted locally (success/failure + reason codes) w/ redaction config default sane
- ✅ exporter schema updated for customers w/ SIEM forwarding
sign-off: Telemetry/audit owner (me) + Kevin (not in thread) for schema sanity.

Hanae Suzuki: key management gating:
- ✅ trust store update mechanism validated in air-gapped flow (customer can add new pubkey file offline)
- ✅ rotation drill completed or explicitly waived w/ documented risk
sign-off: KMS/secrets (me) + Release Eng.

Daniel Carter: evidence detail: pls ensure we store a single “release evidence index” doc that links to:
- signing job run id
- bundle build id
- manifest sha256
- signature file sha256
- SBOM hash
- approver list + timestamps
makes questionnaires way faster.

6) **Audit/telemetry + export schema** (Owner: Telemetry)

- [ ] Exporter mappings updated (if enabled) + redaction defaults reviewed
Sign-off: Logan Wright (+ Kevin Osei async)

7) **Docs + release notes + support readiness** (Owner: Docs/Support/Product)
- [ ] Customer-facing docs updated (verification steps, required installer version)
- [ ] Release notes entry prepared (migration notes + known limitations)
- [ ] Support runbooks + FAQ updated; escalation paths confirmed
Sign-off: Vivek Kulkarni + Dev Patel (+ Docs owner async)

8) **Compliance evidence pack** (Owner: GRC)
- [ ] Evidence index doc created + links to signing logs, manifest/signature hashes, approvers, retention location
Sign-off: Olga Petrov (+ Daniel Carter)

9) **Exception/break-glass** (Only if needed)
- [ ] If break-glass used, approval captured + customer comms + explicit audit markers
Sign-off: Marcus Lin + Dr. Aisha Rahman + Connor O’Brien

If all sign-offs collected -> ✅ RC can be promoted / Private artifacts published.

Sahana Iyer: +1. also suggest adding “manifest sha256 recorded in evidence index” under section 1/8 (we always end up chasing it later).

Vivek Kulkarni: +1 to Sean. also include “required installer version” in release notes gating explicitly.
